rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HERBERT ALBERT SR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues e-mailed to counsel on 03/23/2022 remain unaddressed as of noon on 03/28/2022: 1) Re item 3c of the petition: i) The reappraised value of the real property was $575,000. ii) The box "reappraisal for sale" was not checked on the DE-160 form filed on 2/15/22. iii) The probate referee dated the reappraisal 5/29/22. 2) Item 4c: The reported sale was made (offer was accepted) before the date specified in the published notice of sale as the date on or after which the sale will be made. Probate Code section 10306(a) & LRSF 14.31(B). 3) Item 5b: i) The published notice did not specify the "exclusive" listing given to the broker. LRSF 14.31(B). ii) The ID #1470927 does not appear to belong to Edmond O'Sullivan. 4) Item 5c: No DBA "Coldwell Banker Real Estate" associated with the ID #01908304 appears to be registered. 5) Item 5d: Please provide a copy of the applicable contract provision from which a non-statutory allocation of the commission in the event of a successful overbid can be confirmed. 6) Item 6b: Please provide an amount of estimated annual income from the sale proceeds. 7) Item 7a: The published notice did not specify all the terms of sale set forth on Attachment 4f (items 5-9). Section 10307 & LRSF 14.31(c). 8) Item 8a: (1) must be checked, as the decedent died intestate. 9) The zip code for Evyn Shomer who requested special notice listed on the proof of service appears incorrect. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FERNANDO EXPOSITO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/23/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Courtesy copies of late filed papers just were received on 3/23/22 and 3/28/22. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] A REVISED account is an AMENDED account, not merely a "supplement declaration", which the legislature opted not to recognize in its definitions and terms. [CRC 7.3.] An amendment requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. The executor hired his own wife - and paid her without prior Court approval - for services to the estate on Executor's "behalf". [CRC 7.700.] He alleged in paragraph 18 of his petition that he had not hired any agent who has a family relationship with him. Why are the property management fees so high and varying? Who were the original payees of the reimbursements made from "Bank of America Chk Acct" on 7/9/19 and 7/12/19, and the reimbursements made to the executor on 8/3/20, 9/13/19, 9/18/19, and 8/17/20? Please file the invoices for the $15,000 heater repair on 8/3/20, the eight moving and storage fees paid between 10/19/20 and 12/7/20 totaling $19,682.00, and the travel and hotel fees paid to Sherry Runge totaling exactly $3,000. It is the fiduciary's responsibility to verify that the bank statement is original. If he obtained the original bank statement from the bank, then he must swear that he has the original in his possession. [LRSF 14.34 D.] Has the executor still not been able to identify the source of unidentified deposits totaling $12,835.18 that were received less than one month before he was issued Letters? No receipts have been filed yet for the specifically bequeathed tangible personal property shown on the LOSSES schedule under "Distribution of Real Property". Under what authority did he distribute the tangible personal property? Did he give Notice of Proposed Action? Under what authority did the executor PAY legal fees to Utrecht & Lenvin LLP? [LRSF 14.50.] Pecuniary bequests not made within one year from the date of death bear interest. (Itemized attorney costs should show the date, amount, payee, and description.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/12/22. As noted previously, if the AMENDED accounting petition cannot be filed in time to give 15 days' notice of it after it is filed, Counsel should contact the assigned Examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAHUL BHIDE LIVING TRUST, TRUST CREATED AND EXECUTED NOVEMBER 13, 2012 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Set Aside Order Or Alternatively Clarify Order As Without Prejudice [Ccp 473(B) Discretionary, And Alternatively Ccp 473(C) Mandatory] (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Part 2 of 2 The September 14, 2021 Order denying the Petition was based on the Court's review of the substantive filings by both Petitioner and Objector in this matter: the Petition to Confirm Ownership of Trust Property and Authorize Conveyance; Opposition/Objection to the Petition; Amended Petition to Confirm Ownership of Trust Property and Authorize Conveyance; Supplemental Objection to Amended Petition; Supplemental Declaration of Lalit Balchandani in Support of Amended Petition; and Second Supplemental Objection to Amend Petition to Confirm Ownership of Trust Property and Authorize Conveyance of Trust Property, Request for Continuance and Request for Sanctions, with a supporting attorney declaration.. Having taken a substantive file consisting of the Petition and supporting documents and the Objection and supporting documents under submission, this court acted in accordance with Probate Code section 1046: "The court shall hear and determine any matter at issue and any response or objection presented, consider evidence presented, and make appropriate orders." Petitioner's request in the alternative that the court clarify that the September 14, 2021 Order was entered "without prejudice" is DENIED. Petitioner offers no authority that "with prejudice" is an unwritten default ruling on Probate orders denying petitions. Designation of "with" and "without prejudice" pertains to voluntary and involuntary dismissals, and whether the same claim can be refiled on the same cause of action against the same parties, or whether it is barred by collateral estoppel. A dismissal "without prejudice" means without prejudice to the filing of a new action on the same allegations between the same parties, within whatever applicable statute of limitations (see Cardiff Equities, Inc. v. Superior Court (2008) 83 Cal.Rptr.3d 699; Tokerud v. Capitolbank Sacramento (1995) 45 Cal.Rptr.2d 345). As discussed above, the Sept 14, 2021 Order was not a dismissal order, however (see Civil Code of Procedure sections 581, 581d, 582). The court declines any invitation or request to advise on whether a hypothetical future petition filed by Petitioner or any interested party would or would not be barred by collateral estoppel in this matter. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on March 29, 2022. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. Part 2 of 2

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAHUL BHIDE LIVING TRUST, TRUST CREATED AND EXECUTED NOVEMBER 13, 2012 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Or Alternatively Clarify Order As Without Prejudice [Ccp 473(B) Discretionary, And Alternatively Ccp 473(C) Mandatory]
rulings.Rulings: Part 1 of 2 This tentative is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the Court is Petitioner Lalit Balchandani's Motion to Set Aside Order or Alternatively Clarify Order As Without Prejudice (CCP section 473(b) Discretionary and Alternatively CCP section 473(c) Mandatory. For the reasons set forth below, the Motion is DENIED. On December 28, 2020, Lalit Balchandani, as Trustee, filed Petition to Confirm Ownership of Trust Property and Authorize Conveyance. On March 15, 2021, Objector Daniel Jacob filed Opposition/Objection to the Petition. On May 3, 2021, Petitioner filed an Amended Petition to Confirm Ownership of Trust Property and Authorize Conveyance. On May 10, 2021, Objector filed Supplemental Objection to Amended Petition. On June 13, 2021, Petitioner filed Supplemental Declaration of Lalit Balchandani in Support of Amended Petition. On August 16, 2021, Objector filed Second Supplemental Objection to Amend Petition to Confirm Ownership of Trust Property and Authorize Conveyance of Trust Property, Request for Continuance and Request for Sanctions, with supporting declaration. The hearing on the Petition to Confirm Ownership was originally set for March 24, 2021, then continued to May 12, 2021, then to May 26, 2021, then to June 28, 2021, then to August 2, 2021, and finally to August 23, 2021. At the August 23, 2021 hearing, neither Petitioner nor her attorney appeared. Paul Horcher, counsel for Objector, did appear. Per the hearing Minutes, the court inquired with Mr. Horcher about the sanctions request, and after his presentation to the court, took the Petition under submission, "with the court's intent to deny the petition." On September 14, 2021, the court entered an order denying the Amended Petition. Petitioner brings this motion to set aside the September 14, 2021 Order under Code of Civil Procedure sections 473 (b) and (c), on the argument that it was entered due to counsel's excusable failure to appear at the August 23, 2021 hearing, due to miscalendaring. The Notice of Motion states "Petitioner will move to set aside the default and any judgment thereon taken against Petitioner." Petitioner's Motion to Set Aside Order or Alternatively Clarify Order As Without Prejudice (CCP section 473(b) Discretionary and Alternatively CCP section 473(c) Mandatory is DENIED. Petitioner's arguments for discretionary or mandatory relief under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) are inapplicable to the September 14, 2021 Order. The September 14 Order is not a default order, a default judgment, or a dismissal, and Code of Civil Procedure section 473 does not apply (see Eng. v. IKON Bus. Sols., Inc. (2001) 94 Cal. App. 4th 130, 143-44; Code of Civil Procedure sections 581, 581d, and 582; Probate Code section 1046.) It was not entered due to Petitioner's or her attorney's "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." The Court's September 14, 2021 Order was a judgment on the merits of the Petition. Part 1 of 2

Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be called to admit will. No appearance is required.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WARREN KIM TOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 04/03/2023. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 04/17/2023.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHELSEA JULIA ANNE CURRY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/27/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number must be FILED, conventionally with a cover sheet marked confidential. Examiners will not review unfiled papers. [LRSF 14.7 C.] Limited IAEA authority only limits a personal representative's powers over real property. If all assets are personal property, the amount of bond for a non-resident personal representative must be sufficient to protect all possible claims and is determined by the petitioner's verified declaration, setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate. [LRSF 14.16 F.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/20/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
